findings.problem: The chapter's climactic conflict disperses into parallel compliance demands without forcing an irreversible on-page choice between them, so the causal turn lands as admin traffic rather than a decisive dramatic pivot.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:503-543, stage a forced decision where Ina chooses one gate (network legal vs council policy) or openly defies both, and show immediate retaliation in-scene before the chapter exits.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised 503-543 span contains one explicit choice by Ina that cannot be undone in the same chapter and one immediate concrete consequence (access loss, legal threat, production shutdown, or ally rupture) shown on-page; FAIL if conflict remains only in notices/emails.

findings.problem: Sanitization/moral safety-valve drift: after Ji-eun's bodily crisis is shown, the narrative retreats into portal filing and credo lines, reducing embodied aftermath and cushioning discomfort with principle statements.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:543-573 so aftermath stays scene-level and embodied: include Ina taking a concrete protective action for Ji-eun (medical, legal, or physical extraction) that meets active obstruction and imposes personal cost; trim or reframe slogan-like self-absolution lines.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised 543-573 includes at least one materially risky action by Ina that changes Ji-eun's immediate condition and at least one blocking response from an institution/person on-page; FAIL if the passage resolves mainly through documentation plus abstract principles.

findings.problem: Elliptical clipping in narration (fragment stacks like time-stamp shards and imperative fragments) violates the constitution's no-clipped-narration rule and reads as telegraphic shorthand.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:3 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:386 into fully grammatical narrative clauses while preserving pace through punctuation and sentence length, not dropped function words.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines at 3 and 386 read as complete narrative sentences with explicit subjects and finite verbs (except quoted on-screen text); FAIL if stand-alone clipped narration fragments remain.

findings.problem: Containment-call voices (Yoon, Oh, Choi) converge into similar formal compliance syntax, weakening idiolect separation and making pressure exchanges sound interchangeable.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:503-533, differentiate each speaker's register: Yoon as legal-risk precision, Oh as ideological mandate framing, Choi as talent-economics/brand control, with distinct rhythm and interruption habits.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in revised 503-533 each of Yoon, Oh, and Choi has at least one line whose diction/rhythm is uniquely attributable without dialogue tags and at least one direct rebuttal shifts leverage; FAIL if lines remain cross-swappable.

findings.problem: Kwon's diction over-reuses abstract control tokens (weather/signal/architecture/vector/corridor), reading like mechanical lexical-signature reuse rather than adaptive speech under changing pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Kwon's lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:118-128, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:336-342, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:590-596 so at least part of his pressure language turns concrete (specific legal triggers, rating penalties, personnel consequences) rather than repeating abstraction tokens.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised Kwon dialogue in those spans limits repeated abstraction keywords to one use each and includes at least two concrete consequence statements tied to this production; FAIL if abstraction cluster still dominates.

findings.problem: Recurring emotional-delivery formula (voice + descriptor) appears repeatedly and becomes a prose tic, flattening scene texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:180,210,330,354,406,519,578, replace at least half of voice-led emotional markers with non-vocal carriers (gesture, breath, gaze timing, body tension, spatial movement).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised chapter uses no more than two 'voice + descriptor' emotional cues across those cited lines and adds at least three concrete non-vocal replacements; FAIL if the same cue remains the default.

findings.problem: The repeated 'as if' comparison template appears five times and starts to feel automatic rather than selective.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the constructions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:29,43,198,562,586 so at least three are rendered as direct concrete observations instead of 'as if' similes.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least three of those five lines are rewritten without 'as if' while preserving the same narrative information; FAIL if four or five remain in template form.

findings.problem: A 10+ paragraph run in the edit-bay sequence carries blocking and dialogue but little focalized interior experience, creating screenplay drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:303-333, insert close-third interior beats (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) tied to each decision turn so Ina's cognition stays active inside the production mechanics.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised 303-333 includes at least three explicit interior beats from Ina and no stretch longer than six consecutive paragraphs without interiority; FAIL if the sequence remains primarily external blocking/dialogue.

summary: Chapter 4 hits required plot beats and maintains continuity, but it fails review due to high-severity craft issues (sanitization safety-valve drift and clipped narration) plus unresolved dialogue/prose repetition and interiority problems that flatten voice and reduce climax force.
